blocks.content: I walked to her locker the same way I always walked to her locker. Same hall, same left turn, same morning light coming through the window at the end of the corridor.
No books. No jacket. No anything. The little magnet she kept on the inside of the door — the one she never said anything about but was always there — gone.
I stood there for a second. People moved around me. The hall did what halls do. Nobody stops for this.
He came up slow. Not his regular speed. That told me he already knew I was standing here for a reason.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Maya told me this morning. She heard it from Keisha. I was coming to find you.
He was already next to me. Not in front, not giving a speech — just next to me, looking at the same empty locker.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: ....She used to ask me about you.
I didn't say anything. I just let it sit.

blocks.prompt: She used to ask Jonathan about Larry. How long had she been doing that?

options.label: From the beginning. She saw something and she was patient about it.
Recently. After the steps scenes. After she decided he was worth the wait.
